meta.approach: Tiered testing — pyzbar with enhanced preprocessing, then cv2.QRCodeDetector, then AdvancedCVScanner

barcode_geometric.note: Perspective + cylinder distortion fundamentally breaks bar width ratios. pyzbar cannot recover. Added perspective normalizer, density equalizer, and contour-based correction — limited improvement (+7 images). Custom decoder needed.

damage_noise.note: Improved from 67.3% via heavy denoising + morphological opening. Remaining failures are severe random-damage images where QR finders are destroyed.

edge_tear.note: Large-scale tears/fragments remove too much data. Improved tear detection threshold (250→230) but pyzbar cannot decode images with >40% missing content.

flaw_motion_blur.note: Improved from 80.9% via CLAHE + bilateral filter combo

geometric_perspective.note: cv2.QRCodeDetector added +14.5% on top of pyzbar's 80.0%

ink_corner_destruction.note: Improved from 85.4% via wider adaptive threshold sweep

ink_data_pollution.note: DRAMATIC improvement from 76.2% — enhanced pyzbar with Otsu + bilateral + wide adaptive threshold sweep catches all cases

multi_qr.note: Improved from 68.2% via wider adaptive threshold + morphological operations. Remaining failures are multi-code images where individual QR codes are too small or heavily transformed for finder detection.

unfixable_notes.barcode_geometric: Geometric transformations (perspective 25-70% compression + cylinder curvature) fundamentally break 1D bar/space width ratios. pyzbar requires consistent width ratios for decode. Custom distortion-aware decoder would be needed.
unfixable_notes.edge_tear: Tears/fragments remove >40% of image data. Inpainting cannot reconstruct missing QR modules or barcode bars.
unfixable_notes.damage_noise: Combined random damage (scratches + noise + blur + occlusion) destroys QR finder patterns in ~30% of images.
unfixable_notes.multi_qr: Multi-code images with small (sometimes <50px), transformed QR codes in complex backgrounds exceed current finder detection limits.
